kept.text:
⚾️ MLB Friday Play #3:

𝐆𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐭 𝐇𝐨𝐥𝐦𝐞𝐬 𝐎 𝟒.𝟓 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐤𝐞𝐨𝐮𝐭𝐬 (-𝟏𝟑𝟎 𝐃𝐫𝐚𝐟𝐭𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬)

𝟏𝟎𝟎 💜 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐍𝐄𝐗𝐓 𝐏𝐋𝐀𝐘!

- KENNY POWERS OWNS THE ROCKIES!

- Holmes has DOMINATED this Rockies lineup in 2/2 starts in his career striking out 8 and 15 batters for an 18.48 K/9.

- Colorado bats have been heating up yet they still surrender 10.7 K’s per game over last three, 4th HIGHEST in MLB.

- 4/5 last RHP has CRUSHED this line versus Rockies, including:

Chase Burns: 6.0 IP, 9 K
Freddy Peralta: 5.2 IP, 8 K

- Although Holmes has only exceeded this line once this season, his H2H K% and 5.2 IP projection should be more than enough to get over this 4.5 line!

𝐃𝐑𝐎𝐏 𝐀 💜 𝐈𝐅 𝐓𝐀𝐈𝐋𝐈𝐍𝐆

kept.text:
When sports betting became legal, it got ugly.

Sportsbooks don’t just take bets... they study behavior, target impulses, and profit off addiction. High schoolers are already finding ways in, using parents accounts, chasing that same rush I did.

When I was deep in it, my compulsive patterns weren’t a warning sign to the system... they were an opportunity. I was given free vacations, field passes, golf clubs, all you can drink events… while losing tens of thousands betting every pitch, every at bat, never stepping away from action. It wasn’t entertainment anymore. It was a cycle.

It doesn’t just take money. It takes relationships... Trust... Stability. I drifted, lied, hid things, and lived inside my phone chasing the next bet. I’ve seen how fast it can quietly dismantle a life.

The hardest part is how normal it’s becoming while gambling addiction continues to grow in silence. Most people don’t see what’s happening until it’s already deep.

I’ve personally hit a point where I didn’t think I’d make it out of it, and I know others who’ve been in that same place who tragically took their lives.

If you’re struggling, you’re not alone... there’s support out there. Reach out, my DM's are open.

Blaise ❤️

profile_bio.description: Lost myself to gambling. Found myself in recovery. Sharing the journey so someone else doesn’t feel alone.

DraftKings Daily Grand Slam Payout

Believe it or not, there is about a 55% chance that someone hits a grand slam across today's 15 game slate

Important caveat for this promo: You can play up to a three leg parlay, which is a bit different to what we have been used to lately ‼️

I certainly plan on taking advantage of using all 3 legs.  The bet will probably lose, but it is likely worth the additional chances at a share of this $250,000. 

I listed the top 10 most likely home run hitters, but to be honest, I am afraid that these guys will get overplayed and will mostly avoid them. 

In other words, Judge has less than a 1% chance at hitting a grand slam (probably closer to .5%), but I guarantee much more than 1% of people will play him.

The value lies elsewhere.  Best of luck, and let's see what you have cooked up so far 👇🎰

tweet.text:
🥉0.50u | Mitch Keller o4.5 SO (-140) @ Fanatics, HardRock, Caesars, FanDuel & (-138) @ DraftKings  
🥉0.25u | Mitch Keller o16.5 Pitching Outs (-113) @ DraftKings, (-120) @ Caesars & Bet365  

Mitch Keller has been solid this season for PIT, leading the staff in IP (tied with Skenes) while holding the lowest ERA among PIT starters. His splits are especially notable, as his K% jumps significantly against RHH. He holds just a 16.0% K% vs LHH, but that number rises to 26.5% vs RHH. This is key for this matchup, as CIN is expected to roll out 5 RHH. Keller has also been much more effective against RHH overall, allowing just a .122 AVG compared to .293 vs LHH.

Keller features a nice arsenal, and against RHH he will primarily lean on his 93–95 MPH 4-Seamer, Sweeper, and Sinker. CIN has a 21.0% K% against 93–95 MPH 4-Seamers along with the 7th-highest Whiff%. Against Sweepers, they show major swing-and-miss tendencies with a 35.6% K%, ranking 5th highest. While the Sinker isn’t typically a SO pitch, CIN tends to take 59.0% of Sinkers in the zone and will also chase it outside the zone, often failing to make contact which led to their 6.1% SwStr%.

Against LHH, Keller mixes in his Changeup and Curveball. CIN has struggled heavily against both pitch types, posting a 36.0% K% and 18.1% SwStr% vs Changeups, along with a 37.5% K% and 16.1% SwStr% vs Curveballs. With the projected lineup construction and Keller’s pitch mix aligning well with CIN’s weaknesses, this sets up as a strong opportunity for him to generate SO's.

Follow the unit size & feel free to ask me anything whether it's in dms or the comments 🙏

tweet.text:
5/1 #MLB ⚾️ Play #3 ⤵️

1.1u - Brewers F5 -0.5 (-110 #Draftkings) 

#ThisIsMyCrew vs #NATITUDE 

Also liked and looked at Miz o16.5 Outs and Irvin o2.5 ER but because of odds and MIL offense recently opted for this option.

Both offenses haven’t been great recently, I still give the edge to Milwaukee bats (11th in wRC+ vs RHP compared to Washington’s 23rd). 

Pitcher wise, Miz is clearly the better one to back. Nats have a very lefty-heavy lineup as well which bodes well for Miz who is better against lefties. Irvin on the other hand has allowed 3+ ER in 11 STRAIGHT home starts.
